prompt: Write production Ruby method draw_crawl_space_foundation inside DooleyBuildingSolutions::HouseShellBuilder with operation wrapping, material cache, BIM attribution, and vent interval subtraction.

assertions.text: Code is inside the DooleyBuildingSolutions::HouseShellBuilder module namespace
model.start_operation and model.commit_operation present, with rescue block calling model.abort_operation
rescue clause catches a specific exception class (e.g. Sketchup::Error, StandardError) — NOT a bare `rescue`
@materials_cache hash pattern used — material is not created anew on every call
apply_bim_data called with 'Footing' type for BIM attribution
No use of eval, instance_eval, or shell-out (system/backticks)
Individual method bodies are under 20 lines (long logic extracted into helpers)
